channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Cape Town Muslims Mark Eid al-Fitr with Gratitude, Reflection and Calls for Unity
item.description: As Cape Town’s Muslim community prepares to celebrate Eid al-Fitr following the end of Ramadan, local religious leaders have called for both gratitude and deep reflection. The Ulama Board and the Musl...

item.title: The Australian Government's Role in Empowering Young People Beyond Welfare
item.description: In a recent piece, Jason Falinski critiques the Australian government’s welfare policies and their impact on young people, arguing that they need more than just short-term relief. Falinski highlights ...

item.title: A Critique of Pope Leo's Stance on Artificial Intelligence and Its Global Implications
item.description: The article discusses the unexpected admiration of Pope Leo XIV by the author, despite their past rejection of Catholicism. It focuses on Pope Leo's cautious approach to artificial intelligence (AI), ...

item.title: UK Border Security Chief Departs Amidst Continued Channel Crossings
item.description: Martin Hewitt, the head of the UK's border security command, is stepping down at the end of March, marking another setback for the government's attempts to control the number of small boats crossing t...

item.title: APC Calls on Nigerians to Keep Praying for Peace and Unity during Eid-el-Fitr
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) has sent its heartfelt congratulations to Nigerians, especially the Muslim community, on the joyous occasion of Eid-el-Fitr. In a statement made by the National Pub...

item.title: ANC denies allegations of foreign election interference
item.description: The African National Congress (ANC) has strongly dismissed allegations of foreign involvement in South Africa’s electoral processes. The claims, which surfaced through media reports citing leaked docu...

item.title: Russian recruit flees after discovering deployment to Ukraine instead of Africa
item.description: In late 2025, a young Russian named Georgy Kochkin joined a recruitment programme called the Africa Corps, thinking he would get a well-paid, non-combat job on the African continent. The job posting a...

item.title: Experts advise Osun youths to avoid being used for political violence
item.description: For Osun State, some experts don dey warn youths from Ilobu, Ifon, and Erin-Osun communities make dem no allow any politician use dem take cause wahala again, especially as election season don near. T...

item.title: Liverpool Back Konate Amidst Racist Abuse Following Osimhen Injury
item.description: Liverpool Football Club don react heavily to di racist abuse wey Ibrahim Konate receive online after di match against Galatasaray. Di abuse follow after Konate and Victor Osimhen collide, leading to O...

item.title: Civil case against Gerry Adams discontinued after High Court proceedings
item.description: A civil lawsuit brought against Gerry Adams, the former Sinn Féin leader, over three IRA bombings in Britain has been withdrawn. The claimants, victims of attacks in London and Manchester in 1973 and ...

item.title: Martial arts legend and actor Chuck Norris passes away at 86 surrounded by family
item.description: Chuck Norris, the American martial artist and actor best known for 'Walker, Texas Ranger' and films like 'The Way of the Dragon,' has died aged 86. His family announced his passing on social media, ex...

item.title: Matildas Prepare for Tough Test Against Japan in Asian Cup Final
item.description: The Matildas are gearing up for a challenging showdown against Japan in the 2026 AFC Women's Asian Cup final, set for this Saturday in Sydney. Australia's star defender, Steph Catley, highlighted Japa...

item.title: Former Syrian Colonel Charged with Crimes Against Humanity in UK Court
item.description: Salem Michel Al-Salem, a former colonel in Syria’s Air Force Intelligence Directorate, has appeared in a London court facing charges of crimes against humanity, including murder and torture, linked to...

item.title: Analysing Potential Economic Impacts of the Iran Conflict on Australia
item.description: In the latest episode of 'Back to Back Barries', Barrie Cassidy and Tony Barry dissect the economic repercussions of the ongoing conflict involving Iran, exploring how it could affect Australian house...

item.title: Maritime Workers’ Union of Nigeria Restates Its Political Neutrality Ahead of 2027 General Elections
item.description: The Maritime Workers’ Union of Nigeria (MWUN) don come out again talk say dem go remain neutral as the 2027 general elections dey approach. The President-General of the union, Comrade Francis Bunu, ya...

item.title: MPs Identify Key Operational Failures in SHA Health System
item.description: Members of Parliament's Health Committee has spotlighted serious challenges facing the Social Health Authority (SHA), Kenya's new health insurance scheme meant to replace the old NHIF. During a meetin...

item.title: China seeks deeper climate and green development partnership with Nigeria
item.description: China don reaffirm say e go continue to push forward global climate action and strengthen green development partnership with Nigeria. According to the Chinese Ambassador to Nigeria, Yu Dunhai, China d...

item.title: Possible Human Bone Found in Hackney Linked to 1969 Kidnap-Murder Case of Muriel McKay
item.description: Excavators hired by the family of the late Muriel McKay have uncovered a bone about a metre beneath the ground behind a betting shop in Hackney, East London — a site where they were told her body migh...

item.title: The Legacy of Fink Haysom: A Lifelong Fight for Justice and Reconciliation
item.description: Nicholas 'Fink' Haysom's life was marked by an unwavering commitment to justice, human rights, and reconciliation, from his early days in apartheid South Africa to his later work with the United Natio...

item.title: Iran criticises Australia's stance in Middle East, asylum issue with footballers
item.description: Iran has strongly criticised Australia’s involvement in the Middle East conflict, with Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaell Baghaei accusing Australia of taking the wrong side by aligning wit...
